member.name: M:Polyfills.Polyfill.GetChunks(System.Text.StringBuilder)
member.summary: GetChunks returns ChunkEnumerator that follows the IEnumerable pattern and thus can be used in a C# 'foreach' statements to retrieve the data in the StringBuilder as chunks (ReadOnlyMemory) of characters.

member.name: T:Polyfills.Polyfill.ChunkEnumerator
member.summary: ChunkEnumerator supports both the IEnumerable and IEnumerator pattern so foreach works (see GetChunks). It needs to be public (so the compiler can use it when building a foreach statement) but users typically don't use it explicitly. (which is why it is a nested type).

member.summary: Used to hold all the chunks indexes when you have many chunks.

member.name: T:System.Threading.Lock
member.summary: Provides a way to get mutual exclusion in regions of code between different threads. A lock may be held by one thread at a time.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.Enter
member.summary: Enters the lock. Once the method returns, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock without waiting. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock, waiting for roughly the specified duration. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter(System.Int32)
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock, waiting for roughly the specified duration. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.EnterScope
member.summary: Enters the lock and returns a that may be disposed to exit the lock. Once the method returns, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock. This method is intended to be used along with a language construct that would automatically dispose the , such as with the C# using statement.

member.name: T:System.Threading.Lock.Scope
member.summary: A disposable structure that is returned by , which when disposed, exits the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.Scope.#ctor(System.Threading.Lock)
member.summary: A disposable structure that is returned by , which when disposed, exits the lock.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Cell._runeWidthCache
member.summary: UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth documents the width as (-1, 0, 1, 2). We only need space for these values and a sentinel for uninitialized values. This is only five values in total so we are storing one byte per value. We could store 2 per byte but that would add more logic to the retrieval. We should add one to char.MaxValue because the total number of characters includes \0 too so there are 65536 valid chars.

member.name: T:Spectre.Console.CircularBuffer`1
member.summary: The circular buffer starts with an empty list and grows to a maximum size. When the buffer is full, adding or inserting a new item removes the first item in the buffer.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.CircularBuffer`1.UniqueRemovedCheck
member.summary: We have some debug.asserts to make sure items removed do not still appear in the buffer, but valuetypes can have equal items (or you may want to insert multiple copies of the same instance. This is automatically set to false for valuetypes but you can override.
